summary: A top‑tier soundboard capture with buttery, fat clarity that showcases an early‑'80s masterpiece – smoking Morning Dew, explosive Other One and a tight Shakedown Street set.
review: The Miller SBD of the April 6 1982 show delivers crystal‑clear, full‑bodied audio that many reviewers call “the best release” of this concert. Musically the band is in peak form: Jerry’s guitar flames on Terrapin Station and Morning Dew, Phil’s bass sits prominently in the mix, and the second set rolls through Shakedown Street, Jack A‑Roe and a monster Other One with effortless flow.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive 5‑star comments praise both the performance and the soundboard source; average rating across 17 reviews is ~4.6, with detailed technical and musical observations supporting a high score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners repeatedly note a "fuller, fatter, wider" mix, buttery clarity, Phil’s bass prominent in the mix and virtually no audible flaws; occasional mentions of minor drop‑outs refer to official releases, not this SBD.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers praise the seamless transition into a powerful second set, especially the early‑'80s Spectrum vibe; the show builds energy from a smooth first set into an explosive, tightly connected second half.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Multiple reviewers call his playing "on fire", highlight his Terrapin Station bridge solo and the playful trading licks with Brent on Shakedown.
band_member_comments.Phil: Consistently noted as more prevalent in the mix and delivering a solid, funky bass line throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Praised for lively keyboard work (Brent) that competes for attention; his solos are described as "killing it".
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboards receive glowing remarks, especially during the Shakedown opener and the Terrapin bridge solo.
band_member_comments.Drums: Described as solid and dynamic, with the second‑set drum break noted as particularly strong.
